title: 10 best herbal teas for runny nose

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Kansas Coneflower tea's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and runny nose symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cichoric acid and echinacin, which work to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with a runny nose.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas Coneflower tea's immunomodulatory properties help stimulate the immune system, enhancing its natural response to infections that can cause a runny nose, such as common colds and allergies.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with runny nose because its active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects help stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, which can aid in fighting off underlying infections that may be causing the runny nose. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote a healthier respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms.

Alkaloids (particularly Cichoric Acid): Cichoric acid has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may be contributing to a runny nose. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce nasal congestion.

Triterpenoid Saponins: These compounds have been found to possess immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to allergens and infections, thereby reducing the severity of runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are considered the most effective part for medicinal purposes due to their high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides that help to reduce inflammation and congestion.
Leaves: They contain compounds that help to soothe and calm the respiratory system, making them a popular choice for teas to alleviate a runny nose.
Stems: They also contain medicinal compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nose and sinuses, although they may be less potent than roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh roots or 10-15 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall season.
Clean the echinacea roots under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris thoroughly.
Chop the echinacea roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 teaspoon of chopped echinacea roots with 1 cup of boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the echinacea solids completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like allantoin, which help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating a runny nose.
Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Plantain's antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause a runny nose, such as the common cold or sinusitis.
Mucilages and demulcents: The mucilages and demulcents in Plantain tea create a soothing, protective barrier on the nasal membranes, helping to calm irritation and reduce excess mucus production.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, teas helps with runny nose because of its natural expectorant properties. The plantain plant contains mucilages, which are soothing compounds that help thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages. When consumed as a tea, these mucilages work to reduce inflammation and congestion, promoting a healthy flow of mucus and relieving nasal congestion. This makes plantain tea a popular natural remedy for colds and allergies. Its decongestant properties also make it an effective aid in managing sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of runny nose by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent.
Aucubin: A phenylethanoid glycoside that has been shown to exhibit antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used in teas due to their expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and runny nose.
Seeds: Plantago major seeds are used in teas for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
Stems: The stems of Plantago major are used in teas to provide anti-catarrhal properties, which can help to reduce mucus and alleviate congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves in late spring or early summer when they are at their peak potency.
Chop 2-3 teaspoons of plantago major leaves into fine pieces using sharp scissors or a knife.
Steep the chopped leaves in 8 oz of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before drinking it slowly and mindfully.

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Cleaver tea's astringent properties help to reduce excessive mucus production and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in cleaver tea may help to alleviate symptoms of rhinitis (inflammation of the mucous membranes) and reduce nasal discharge.
Expectorant properties: Cleaver tea's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the respiratory tract, providing temporary relief from a runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Galium aparine, also known as cleavers, teas helps with runny nose because it has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, work to reduce nasal congestion and swelling, alleviating the symptoms of a runny nose. Additionally, cleavers' astringent properties help to dry up excess mucus, promoting a clearer nasal passage and relieving the discomfort associated with runny nose. As a result, Galium aparine tea may offer a natural relief from nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and swelling, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms.

Triterpenoids: These compounds possess antihistamine-like properties, which can counteract the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with allergies.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used to make teas for runny nose due to their antihistamine and decongestant properties.
Stems: Stems are used in teas for runny nose as they possess astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal discharge.
Flowers: Flowers are also used in teas for runny nose due to their decongestant and antiseptic properties, which help alleviate congestion and soothe the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems for tea in late spring or early summer when they are most potent.
Dry the harvested galium aparine in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 1-2 hours.
Steep 2 teaspoons of dried galium aparine in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Serve the herbal tea hot or iced within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Dandelion tea's antihistamine and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce nasal congestion, relieve sinus pressure, and alleviate allergies-related symptoms like a runny nose.
Expectorant Properties: The tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing the sensation of a runny nose.

Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion tea's antioxidants help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to congestion, sinus pressure, and a runny nose by reducing free radicals that exacerbate these symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with runny nose because of its anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The roots and leaves of the dandelion plant contain compounds that have natural antihistamine effects, helping to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure. Additionally, the tea's diuretic properties help to flush out excess mucus and fluids, providing relief from a runny nose and promoting overall respiratory health. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating runny nose symptoms.
Apigenin: A flavonoid phenolic compound, apigenin has been shown to exhibit antihistaminic and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help relieve allergic reactions and reduce nasal discharge.

Taraxasterol acetate: Similar to taraxasterol, this triterpenoid saponin also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and runny nose by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part used for teas to combat runny nose due to their high concentration of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make teas due to their high content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which help reduce inflammation and relieve nasal congestion.
Roots: Roots are used to make teas as they contain inulin, a prebiotic that can help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon.
Clean the harvested leaves and flowers by gently swishing them in cold water for 2 minutes.
Chop the cleaned taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped taraxacum officinale in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.

remedy_properties: Antihistaminic properties: Lemon verbena tea helps to reduce histamine levels, which can alleviate nasal congestion and relieve a runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, leading to a decrease in mucus production and runny nose symptoms.
Decongestant properties: Lemon verbena tea contains volatile oils that can help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and providing relief from a stuffy or runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena officinalis, also known as lemon verbena, teas helps with runny nose because of its natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea contains compounds like verbascoside, which helps to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages. Additionally, Verbena officinalis has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, allowing them to heal and recover from irritation and infection. As a result, consumption of this tea can provide relief from runny nose and other respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Verbascoside: This iridoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose symptoms.

Rosmarinic acid: As a phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing excessive mucus production.
Verbenaoside: This iridoid glycoside has been shown to possess antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate allergic rhinitis symptoms, including runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in volatile oils, which provide the medicinal properties needed to soothe a runny nose.
Flowers: The flowers contain a high concentration of essential oils, which help to decongest and calm the nasal passages.
Roots: The roots of the plant are known to contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal flavor and aroma.
Rinse the harvested verbena officinalis leaves and flowers with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried verbena officinalis leaves or one tablespoon of fresh leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the verbena officinalis mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the verbena officinalis leaves and flowers.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
Expectorant: The menthol content in peppermint tea acts as an expectorant, loosening and thinning mucus, making it easier to expel, thus reducing the runniness of the nose.
Decongestant: Peppermint's natural decongestant properties help reduce blood flow to the nasal tissues, decreasing swelling and reducing mucus production, which in turn alleviates a runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to thin mucus and clear nasal passages, providing instant relief from congestion. The tea's cooling sensation also helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, peppermint's expectorant properties help to loosen and expel mucus, making it a popular natural remedy for soothing a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as a decongestant by thinning mucus and promoting drainage, which helps relieve a runny nose.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, a terpene, has natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita to make teas due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which help to decongest the nasal passages.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita also contain essential oils that can help to relieve congestion and open up airways, making them a popular choice for teas.
Flowers: While not as commonly used as leaves or stems, the flowers of Mentha x piperita can still be used to make teas that help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers from a garden or store.
Clean 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers with cold water for 10 minutes.
Chop the clean mentha x piperita leaves and flowers into small pieces with a knife.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped mentha x piperita in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the steeped mentha x piperita tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove solids.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to your runny nose.
Expectorant properties: The saponins in thyme tea help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure, further alleviating a runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, teas helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The tea's expectorant properties also aid in loosening and clearing mucus, providing relief from a runny nose and promoting a more comfortable breathing experience. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Thymus vulgaris are the most used part in teas for runny nose due to their high content of thymol, which has decongestant properties.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are also commonly used in teas to help alleviate runny nose symptoms due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are sometimes used in teas to help relieve runny nose, as they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and ease congestion.
remedy_recipe: Choose high-quality thymus vulgaris leaves with a strong aroma and minimal moisture content.
Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried thymus vulgaris leaves for every 8 ounces of water used.
Heat 8 ounces of water to a rolling boil in a teapot for 2-3 minutes.
Steep the dried thymus vulgaris leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to release oils.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and serve immediately.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage tea contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, which can alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols present in sage tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal tissues, making it easier to breathe and reducing mucus production.

The herb contains compounds like thujone and camphor, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. As the tea is consumed, these compounds work to clear excess mucus and reduce the production of histamine, a chemical that can exacerbate nasal congestion. remedy_constituents: Ursolic Acid: A phenolic acid found in Salvia officinalis that helps reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating runny nose symptoms.

Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic diterpene acid that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose by preventing the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.

Carnosic Acid: A phenolic diterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and infection that may contribute to runny nose, and also exhibit antioxidant properties to protect against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for runny nose, as they contain bioactive compounds such as thujone and camphor, which possess anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
Roots: Used to make teas for runny nose, as they contain compounds like salvin and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to combat respiratory issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh salvia officinalis leaves from mature plants in the morning for optimal flavor and aroma.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cold water to remove dirt and debris from the plant surface.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the plant material from the liquid.
Serve the herbal tea immediately and enjoy as a warm or chilled beverage within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Decongestant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal teas like peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger contain decongestant and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce nasal congestion and swelling, relieving a runny nose.

Antihistamine properties: Teas made from herbs like chamomile, lemon balm, and passionflower have antihistamine properties that can help reduce the body's histamine response, which can contribute to a runny nose and other allergy symptoms.

Expectorant and mucolytic properties: Teas like thyme, eucalyptus, and slippery elm contain expectorant and mucolytic compounds that help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and discomfort associated with a runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, teas helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The flavonoids and amino acids present in the tea help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. The catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have been shown to have a positive effect on the immune system, helping to combat infections that cause runny noses. Additionally, the theanine content in tea promotes relaxation and reduces stress, which can exacerbate nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These phenolic compounds help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating runny nose symptoms.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid and a powerful antioxidant, quercetin helps to reduce inflammation and stabilize the mucus membranes, thereby preventing excessive mucus production and runny nose.

Theaflavins: These polyphenolic compounds help to reduce inflammation and improve the nasal mucosa's natural barrier function, preventing the entry of allergens and irritants that can cause runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for runny nose due to their natural decongestant properties.
Buds: Used to make teas for runny nose due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Stems: Used to make teas for runny nose due to their ability to help reduce nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the camellia sinensis leaves in the early morning when they are still moist with dew and have the best flavor profile.
Rinse the leaves in cold running water to remove dirt and impurities and bring out their natural flavors.
Heat the water to a temperature of 160-170°f for green tea and 200-212°f for black tea.
Steep the tea leaves in the hot water for 1-3 minutes depending on the desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves to enjoy your freshly brewed camellia sinensis tea.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and runny nose.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of licorice tea help loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to expel from the body and reducing congestion and runny nose.
Antiviral and antimicrobial activities: Licorice tea has antiviral and antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections caused by viruses and bacteria, which can contribute to a runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the licorice plant contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which soothe and calm the nasal passages. This helps to reduce inflammation and congestion, making it easier to breathe and alleviating runny nose symptoms. Additionally, licorice tea's demulcent properties help to protect the mucous membranes, further reducing irritation and discomfort associated with a runny nose. remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with runny nose by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators, such as histamine and leukotrienes, which contribute to nasal congestion and discharge.

Licoricidin: This flavanone glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and runny nose by blocking the action of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.

Licorisoflavan A: This isoflavan has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce nasal inflammation and congestion, thereby alleviating symptoms of runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are commonly used to make teas due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to help alleviate congestion.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used to make teas, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin, which can still provide relief from runny nose and other respiratory issues.

Buds: Buds are occasionally used to make teas, as they are thought to have a similar composition to roots but with a milder effect, making them a suitable option for those who prefer a gentler remedy.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and place them in a clean tea infuser.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a kettle to 212 degrees fahrenheit for 5 minutes.
Steep the glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the roots.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving and consuming immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with runny nose because its active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, these compounds help to relax the nasal passages and reduce swelling, allowing for easier breathing and a decrease in mucus production. Additionally, ginger's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, providing quick relief from congestion and sinus pressure. As a result, drinking herbal ginger teas can provide natural and soothing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce nasal inflammation and congestion by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Decongestant and expectorant properties: The volatile oils present in ginger tea, like cineole and borneol, have a decongestant effect, helping to reduce excess mucus and ease breathing, while also acting as an expectorant to help loosen and clear out stubborn mucus.

Antihistamine-like effects: Ginger's bioactive compounds, such as paradol and zingiberen, exhibit antihistamine-like properties, which can help alleviate allergy symptoms like a runny nose by blocking the action of histamine on nasal tissues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems of the ginger plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms of runny nose.
Barks: Ginger barks contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have natural antihistamines and decongestant effects, making them effective in relieving a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginger roots from a trusted supplier or prepare fresh roots for drying according to your preference.
Measure one teaspoon of dried ginger powder or three-inch piece of fresh ginger root and place it in a teapot.
Add boiling water to the teapot and let it steep for five to seven minutes to allow flavors and active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard them properly.
Enjoy the warm and soothing zingiber officinale tea, which can be consumed up to three times a day as needed.

Expectorant properties: The volatile oils in clove tea, like camphor and cineole, can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of a runny nose.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10g of dried syzygium aromaticum leaves and flowers from a trusted source or store.
Rinse the herbs gently with filtered water to remove any impurities or debris.
Combine the rinsed herbs with 200ml of boiling distilled water in a large glass teapot.
Steep the herbal mixture for 5-7 minutes before straining and cooling it down to room temperature.
Store the prepared tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

Expectorant properties: Wild marjoram's essential oils can stimulate the cough reflex and help loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and runny nose.

Leaves: Leaves contain natural decongestants and antihistamines, which help to reduce swelling and relieve pressure in the sinuses, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried oregano leaves from your garden or store-bought source.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried oregano leaves and place it in a teapot.
Heat one cup of boiling water in the kettle and pour it over the oregano leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the tea to infuse with its medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or piece of cheesecloth before drinking.

Rhyzomes: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes can soothe and calm irritated nasal tissues, reducing excess mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and pour boiling water over it.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Add honey to taste if desired adjusting the sweetness level to individual preference.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption drinking it warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with runny nose because its active compounds, such as apiolin and chamazulene, have natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties help to reduce congestion and relieve nasal passages of excess mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a clearer sense of smell. Additionally, yarrow's ability to soothe and calm the mucous membranes can also help to alleviate sinus pressure and discomfort, providing welcome relief from colds and allergies.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Yarrow's antibacterial compounds help to combat underlying infections that can cause a runny nose, reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Astringent properties: The astringent properties in yarrow help to constrict blood vessels and reduce mucus production, providing immediate relief from a runny nose and sinus pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion, itchiness, and discomfort associated with a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing nasal congestion and alleviating sinus pressure.
Flowers: The flowers of the yarrow plant contain flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers by pinching off clusters from mature plants.
Rinse the harvested plant material with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the plant material into small pieces using scissors or a pair of pruning shears to release oils.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of chopped achillea millefolium in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to infuse the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with runny nose because its natural properties calm and soothe irritated nasal passages, reducing congestion and inflammation. The tea's antiviral and antibacterial compounds work to combat infections and allergies that can cause excessive mucus production, while its calming effects on the body help to regulate respiratory functions, promoting a clearer breathing and sinus relief. This holistic approach allows for effective and gentle relief from runny nose symptoms without harsh medications or chemicals.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce histamine levels in the body, thereby alleviating nasal congestion and runny nose symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm tea, such as caffeic acid and hesperidin, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, providing relief from runny nose.

Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea's antimicrobial properties can help to combat infections that may be contributing to a runny nose, such as sinusitis or allergies, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain essential oils that help soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing congestion and runniness.
Flowers: The fragrant flowers of lemon balm have anti-inflammatory properties that can ease sinus pressure and relieve a stuffy nose.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat filtered water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil then remove from heat.
Pour the hot water over the measured melissa officinalis leaves in a teapot or infuser and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to catch any loose herb particles.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then drink immediately while warm and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The flavonoids present in lavender, particularly luteolin and apigenin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. When consumed as a tea, these compounds help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes, reducing excessive mucus production and promoting healthy drainage. This results in a significant reduction in runny nose symptoms, allowing for easier breathing and improved overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving congestion and runniness.

Antihistamine properties: The antihistamine compounds present in lavender tea, including apigenin and geraniol, can help alleviate allergic reactions that trigger a runny nose by blocking histamine receptors and reducing inflammation.

Sedative and relaxant properties: Lavender tea's calming effects on the body can help soothe and calm irritated nasal tissues, reducing mucus production and promoting healthy drainage of the sinuses, thereby relieving a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing congestion and runny nose.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool, which has natural decongestant properties to ease sinus pressure and relieve runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Rinse the flowers gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 200ml boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool before serving, as it can be consumed hot or cold at a dosage of one cup per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties, which work to clear out excess mucus and reduce nasal congestion. The herb's essential oils, particularly camphor and borneol, have anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe the mucous membranes and calm down the nasal passages. Additionally, rosemary tea's antioxidant-rich compounds may also help boost the immune system, further aiding in the relief of a runny nose by reducing inflammation and fighting off underlying infections.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Rosemary tea's antihistamine properties help to reduce histamine release, which can ease nasal congestion and relieve a runny nose by reducing inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, have natural anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated nasal tissues, reducing redness, swelling, and excess mucus production.

Expectorant properties: The essential oils present in rosemary tea, like camphor and limonene, act as expectorants, helping to loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, sinuses, and throat, providing relief from a runny nose and sinus congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have astringent properties, which help to reduce congestion and inflammation in the nasal passages.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds contain carnosic acid, a compound that has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective against respiratory infections and runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves into a teapot or infuser.
Heat 8 ounces of boiling water and pour it over the rosemary leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Add honey to taste if desired before drinking the tea immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The tea's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion, providing relief from sinus pressure and excess mucus production. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out trapped mucus, leaving the nose feeling clearer and more decongested. As a natural remedy, stinging nettle teas offer a gentle and effective solution for alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
Natural decongestant: The natural vasodilatory properties of nettle tea can help to open up airways and sinuses, reducing congestion and mucus production, thereby alleviating a runny nose.

Antihistamine-like effects: Nettle tea has been shown to have antihistamine-like effects, which means it can help reduce histamine release, a common allergen trigger that contributes to nasal congestion and a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, stinging nettle roots help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Leaves: Rich in vitamins A and C, stinging nettle leaves support immune function and help to fight off infections that can cause runny noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried stinging nettle leaves measuring one teaspoon per cup of water.
Combine harvested leaves with boiling water in a large ceramic teapot for five minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a glass container discarding solids instantly.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 15-20 minutes before serving hot or chilled.
Drink two cups of prepared nettle tea twice daily as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with runny nose because it contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds that provide natural relief from congestion. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, while its expectorant properties loosen and clear out excess mucus. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose. As a result, Tasmanian blue gum teas can provide fast and effective relief from a runny nose, allowing you to breathe easily and feel more comfortable.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains antiviral compounds like eucalyptol, which helps combat viral infections that can cause runny noses by reducing inflammation and killing viruses.
Decongestant properties: The tea's decongestant properties help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure by shrinking blood vessels in the nose and sinuses, thereby relieving a runny nose.

Expectorant action: Tasmanian Blue Gum's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel excess mucus and reducing the sensation of a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce nasal congestion and ease sinus pressure.
Barks: Containing tannins, which have natural astringent properties that help dry up excess mucus and soothe irritated nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves or four teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the eucalyptus globulus leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for eight to ten minutes to allow the herbal properties to extract.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or tea ball and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for three to five minutes before consumption, then drink slowly and enjoy.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal teas are congestion, sinusitis, and allergies. Herbal teas such as peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger have natural decongestant properties that help relieve nasal passages and ease breathing. Other herbs like nettle, chamomile, and lemon balm also provide relief from allergic reactions and sinus pressure, helping to soothe the throat and calm irritated mucous membranes.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for runny nose are a natural remedy that helps alleviate congestion and soothe the nasal passages. These teas are made from herbs such as peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger, which have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. Drinking these teas can help reduce mucus production, ease breathing, and provide relief from sinus pressure. Examples include peppermint tea, eucalyptus tea, and ginger tea, which can improve quality of life by reducing the frequency and severity of runny nose episodes.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with runny nose is a blend of Echinacea, Peppermint, and Eucalyptus. Echinacea is known for its immune-boosting properties, which help to combat the underlying infection causing the runny nose. Peppermint tea helps to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion. Eucalyptus tea, with its decongestant properties, further aids in clearing the nasal passages and relieving sinus pressure, making this trio an effective natural remedy for runny nose.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine (New York, N.Y.), researchers conducted a 90-day double-blind placebo-controlled study to evaluate the efficacy of an echinacea compound herbal tea preparation, known as Echinacea Plus, in relieving cold and flu symptoms. In this study, 95 subjects with early symptoms of cold or flu, including runny nose, were randomly assigned to receive either Echinacea Plus tea or a placebo, with the experimental group consuming five to six cups per day for five days. The results showed a significant difference between the two groups, with the experimental group experiencing a shorter duration of symptoms and improved recovery compared to the placebo group, indicating that herbal teas like Echinacea Plus may be effective in alleviating runny nose and other cold symptoms.
